heroHeadline: Chad Commits 1,500 Troops to Haiti GSF; 803 Kenyan MSS Officers Withdrawn as Transition Reaches Final Phase
heroSubtitle: Chad announced a 1,500-troop deployment to Haiti's Gang Suppression Force on April 20, 2026 — the largest single national contribution — with ~400 Chadian soldiers already on the ground and two battalions of 750 each committed for one year under UN Resolution 2793. The advance GSF force now stands at ~545 personnel (400 Chadian + 70 Salvadoran + 75 Guatemalan), targeting a full 5,550-strong force by October 2026. Kenya's MSS drawdown entered its final phase on April 21 with 150 more officers departing, bringing total Kenyan withdrawals to 803. Haiti and the Dominican Republic confirmed May 1 as the date for the first commercial flights between the two countries in over two years, connecting Cap-Haïtien to Dominican airports. The UN Security Council held its quarterly Haiti briefing on April 21, with BINUH chief Carlos Ruiz Massieu reporting on the security-election dilemma: voter registration remains indefinitely postponed, threatening the August 30 election date, while 23 communes remain inaccessible to security forces. The 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan remains only ~20% funded ($172M of $880M needed) as USAID cuts deepen.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, Jamaica Gleaner, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, JURIST, International Rescue Committee, NPR, NBC News, Capital FM Kenya, Dominican Today, Security Council Report
